title: The Many Adventures of Mistress Mouse
summary: Mistress Mouse is a resourceful mechanic and racer always ready for a new challenge. From using her tow truck to rescue Olivia Mouse’s car to building sprawling Lego towns with her friend Lily, she is a pillar of her community. Her adventures take her on a sunny vacation to the museums of Mexicota and onto the race track, where she pilots her custom five-wheeled purple car to victory against a fleet of tow trucks and a school bus. Even a broken merry-go-round at the amusement park is no match for her skills, as she uses a hammer to dislodge a stray nut bar and save the day. Whether she's racing through the jungle or fixing rides, Mistress Mouse is the hero of every story.

highlightQuote.text: It was a piece of nut bar.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: More stars in store. Okay, how about we do one about...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mistress Mouse!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Mistress Mouse meets a dinosaur?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No! Mistress Mouse pulls out Olivia's car.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Again? Okay, this one's called Mistress Mouse pulls out Olivia's car again. So what happened first?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So the car, and she went over it and she pulled out, and it went...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It went pop. And then what did Olivia Mouse do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She drove in her car and then, and then the problem was all fixed.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All fixed. Okay, let's do a new story about somebody else.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, no. We're going to do the same story about Mistress Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Once upon a time, Mistress Mouse was driving her tow... Once upon a time, Mistress Mouse was driving her tow truck, and then...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What? What happened?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Nothing got stuck. So she just kept driving and it was a beautiful warm sunny day, and she was driving along the road and suddenly she noticed something. What did she notice?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She didn't notice anything. But she felt a little hungry so she decided to stop somewhere for lunch. So where did she stop?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She stopped at the diner!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, she stopped at the diner, that's a great place for lunch. And so she went in the diner and what did she do then?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She ate some bacon!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Bacon. Did she have anything else with her bacon?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Like fruit. Fruit, oh, and potato and ketchup.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that sounds like a delicious meal. Pepper and salt too. Oh, did she drink anything?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, she drank some leche.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, delicious. And then what did she do when she was done eating?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She went back outside to her, to her tow truck.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, but then as soon as she got in her tow truck, she got a phone call. Bring, bring, bring! And she picked up the phone and she said, Mistress Mouse here, who's calling? And who was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was her friend Lily.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and Lily was calling. What did Lily say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She said, do you want to have a little chat with me and my little brothers Oscar and Alfie and Arthur and Victor?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mistress Mouse said, yes, I'd love to have a little chat, maybe I could come over and visit. Can I come over?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, you can!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, well I just had a nice meal, so I'll drive right over to your house. And so Mistress Mouse started up her truck, vroom, and she drove over to Lily's house. And when she got there, she went inside and what did she see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She saw her brother Arthur, Victor, Oscar and Alfie and Victor.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and what were they doing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They were, they were playing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What were they playing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They were playing with Legos!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh Legos, Mistress Mouse came in and said, I love Legos, what are you all building with your Legos today?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm building a bridge.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A bridge. Is it a big bridge?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A small bridge.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What color is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's orange.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mistress Mouse said, could I help build? Maybe I'll build a road to go right next to the bridge. Maybe I could lie down, father.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That, but you keep telling stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. So then Mistress Mouse had a really nice time playing Legos with her friend Lily and all Lily's brothers and sisters and they built a Lego bridge and Lego roads and Lego cars and Lego buildings, they built a whole Lego town with a Lego forest and Lego airplanes and a Lego zoo with Lego animals and a Lego pond and they had such a good time. And then by that time it was time for Lily and her family to have dinner and for Mistress Mouse to go home and have dinner at her house. So Mistress Mouse got back in her tow truck and she drove all the way home. The end.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: More stories about Mischief Mouse!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Um, Mischief Mouse or Mistress Mouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mistress Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Once upon a time, is your bum okay?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Once upon a time, um, Mistress Mouse decided to go on a vacation. And so she drove her truck really far away to a beautiful place that was nice and warm and sunny. It was...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was called Mexicota.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mexicota. It had museums in it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It had museums, what was in the museum? It had so much things that used to play with, reeds and play with.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And, and they had stars too and blocks.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They had stars. Now, when Mistress Mouse went into the museum in Mexicota, what was the first thing she saw in the museum?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Books.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Books, oh. And so she went over to the bookshelf and said I love books and she pulled a book off the shelf. What book was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was Dinosaur Dance.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Dinosaur Dance, she said I love this one and she started to read Dinosaur Dance. Do you remember how that book goes?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. Can you say any part of it? No, do we have that book?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That one is at the library. That one has, um, the stegosaurus goes shimmy shimmy shake and lots of other parts that I don't remember.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mommy remembers them.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, Mommy probably remembers them, she's good at that kind of thing. And then Mistress Mouse explored the next part of the museum and what did she find?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She found blocks.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: More blocks? So in the next room she pulled another book off the shelf, what was this one about? No, no, no, she didn't pull books off. Oh, what did she find?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She found blocks!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh blocks. What kind of blocks?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Orange blocks, a blue block and red block.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And did she build something out of them?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, she built a tall tower.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then what did she do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She did mischief.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mischief? Did she smash the tower?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes! Boom!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now let's do some stories about Mistress Mouse in her race car.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Mistress Mouse in her race car. Once upon a time Mistress Mouse woke up and she heard an announcement on the radio and it said, today, today, today, a big car race, anyone can enter, the fastest car wins. And so she said to herself, hmm, maybe I could come to town today and enter the car race because I have a fast car. So what did she do first?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She got a race car!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She went into her garage and she went to her little race car. So today instead of driving her tow truck, she'll drive the race car. Tell me about the race car.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was purple and pink.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was purple and pink. And how many wheels did it have?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It had five wheels.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It had five wheels, wow. Um, did it have any decorations on it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nope, it was plain. But it had one decoration.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh what was the decoration, was it a picture?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nope, it was a, it was some tape that taped a picture of a collage. No, no, the decoration was a collage.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh her race car had a collage taped onto it, that sounds awesome. So she started up her race car, vroom, and she said I think I can win this race. And so she drove into town to find the starting line of the race, vroom vroom vroom vroom, and she drove up and she saw that a lot of her friends were also entering the race with their race cars. Who else was joining the race?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know. Mischief Mouse!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh Mischief Mouse was joining the race, what did Mischief Mouse have for a race car?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She had borrowed Mistress Mouse's tow truck.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh Mischief Mouse borrowed Mistress Mouse's tow truck to enter the race. And who else was in the race? Was Talula in the race?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Talula's not a mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's true, Talula is a bird. Um, was Charlie in the race?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nope. Victor was in the race.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh what was Victor driving?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She was driving a nice tow truck.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh Victor was driving a tow truck also, wow. Was Lily in the race?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What was Lily driving?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She was driving a pink one. She was driving a truck.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A truck, oh cool. Was Oscar in the race?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, he was.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Oscar had an amazing vehicle, it was different from everyone else's, what was Oscar's vehicle?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was a school bus.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh Oscar was driving a school bus. And was Alfie in the race too?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Alfie was in the race too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what was Alfie driving?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She was driving a nice pink tow truck.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There were so many tow trucks in the race. And then the announcer said, okay, it's almost time for the big race. Um, everyone drive up to the starting line and then vroom vroom vroom all the tow trucks and Oscar's school bus and Mischief Mouse's tow truck and Mistress Mouse's race car vroom vroom vroom they all drove up and got in a line at the starting line of the race. And the announcer said, okay, today's race is a long race, you're going to drive all the way to the pond and around the pond and then you're going to drive through the desert and through the jungle and over the mountains and then you'll get to the finish line and the first one there wins. And I'll say ready set go and then you'll start. Okay, start your engines, and then vroom everyone started the engines of their cars. And the announcer said, ready, set, go! And then vroom vroom vroom vroom all the cars zoomed off down the race track.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What else did the announcer say?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well then after the cars started the announcer said, and look it's Oscar in his school bus off to an early lead zooming out front of everyone else. But then Mistress Mouse in her little tiny race car is vrooming out ahead of Oscar vroom vroom vroom vroom as she speeds up and passes him. But Mischief Mouse is close behind in her tow truck vroom vroom vroom vroom. They're all in a tight pack, it's a close race everyone as we approach the pond vroom vroom vroom vroom.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And what else does he say?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, um, maybe you could say.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I know, I don't know. But I can tell my own story or you could continue the race one.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I like when you tell your own story. Do you want to tell yours?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nope, not right now. But Dad you have to keep on the race story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh okay, well we do need to get to the end of the race. So then all the race cars vroomed around the pond and they vroomed through the desert and they vroomed through the, what else did I say, the jungle? And then finally was the last part of the race. They went vroom vroom vrooming up the mountain and it was a steep mountain so all the cars had to slow down and they were working hard puff puff to get up the mountain. And then vroom vroom vroom who was the first one to get to the top of the mountain?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mischief Mouse!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mischief Mouse in her borrowed tow truck was the first to the top of the mountain. But everyone else was close behind and so as they went over the top and started to go down the mountain to the finish line they all started to go faster and faster as they rolled downhill. And Oscar Mouse and Victor Mouse and Mischief Mouse and Mistress Mouse were all neck and neck, they were all so close vroom vroom vroom as they went so fast and they were almost at the finish line and just at the last moment Mistress Mouse pulled ahead by a nose and vroomed across the finish line first and she was the winner and everyone cheered, hooray for Mistress Mouse!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mistress, the stars, where are the stars?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'll fix it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The stars are not here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Izzy it's time to tell, I think we have time for like two more stories or one story and one book, what do you think?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe one more story about the race.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, one more story about, do you want to tell one?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, not right now. You want Daddy to start it? Yep. What should this one be about?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Other Mouse!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: How about I put your shirt on you? Not right now. Okay I'll wait. Other Mouse. Once upon a time Other Mouse decided he was going to join a very different kind of race. This one was a race where you jog. Do you know what jogging is?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. Jogging is where you, um, run. Nope, nope, you drive in the race.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Other Mouse decided to drive in the race. And so he went to the race course and this was a circular race, you go around and around and around and around as fast as you can and you have to go around three times. And Other Mouse got there and there were lots of other mice to race against him and he thought oh I... No no no you don't go around and round, you drive through the jungle then the pond and then you drive over the mountain and then you drive over the zoo and you drive over the, and then you drive over the, then you drive over the lake. Oh over the lake, that's how you get to the finishing line.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow that sounds like an amazing race. So Other Mouse decided to enter that race and so he drove drove drove drove drove and he went over the mountain and over the pond and over the zoo and through the jungle and over the mountain again and vroom vroom vroom vroom and then he made it to the finish line and he finished the race.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: One more story for me to go.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well it's getting close to be time for bed. So...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: When will mommy come again?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mommy will come in five or ten minutes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Is she pumping? Not yet. Is she pumping? I don't think so, I think she's gonna pump in a little while. So we're gonna have to put on your shirt and your diaper.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I don't want to put on those. Okay well we don't have to do it yet. Do you want to switch over to reading some books?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I want tow truck with... Oh Mistress. Hi, it looks like someone needs diaper. Yeah, we didn't do diaper yet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Are you ready? Yeah. Okay, let's get diaper going. What are you going to do? Oh Mom, the stars are blocking the way. Oh let me move it, now you can see. Do you want to do one more story with Daddy or do you want to read a book?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want one more story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay Daddy can you do one more story while I get Izzy dressed?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. Once upon a time Mistress Mouse woke up in her house and she got a phone call, ring ring ring, she picked up the phone she said, Hello Mistress Mouse Repair Services, what can I do for you? Um, and the person calling said, Hello this is Joey Giraffe, I work at the amusement park and our merry-go-round is stuck, can you come and fix it Mistress Mouse? And she said, Yeah I can help with that. And so she got in her tow truck and she drove to the amusement park, vroom vroom vroom vroom, and she got there and she saw all of the rides were spinning and turning around and all the people were playing the games and eating the food except in one place there was a giraffe standing there next to the merry-go-round and it was not moving. And the giraffe said, Mistress Mouse can you help us out please? And she said, Okay let's see what the problem is. And she went over to the merry-go-round and what did she notice about it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was broken. It wasn't going. The little motor was trying to turn it and it was going chugga-chugga-chugga-chugga but it was stuck because there was something stuck inside the merry-go-round and Mistress Mouse said, Aha, I think I see the problem. She looked inside and what did she see stuck inside the merry-go-round?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was a piece of nut bar.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was a piece of nut bar. Mistress Mouse peered inside with her flashlight and she said, Oh I think I see the problem, there's a piece of nut bar inside there. And the giraffe said, A piece of nut bar, that's ridiculous, how are you going to get it out? And Mistress Mouse said, I'll have to go get my toolkit. She went back to her truck and she got a tool to help her, what did she get?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She got a hammer!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She got a hammer and she reached in with her hammer, bang bang bang, and then she pulled out the nut bar, pluck! And then pop, it popped right out. And then she said, Okay Joey Giraffe, try it again. And he switched the lever, vroom chugga-chugga-chugga-chugga, and then the giant beautiful merry-go-round roared to life and it could spin around again and everyone was so happy!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Daddy, have more stories?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That was the last story Izzy, would you like Mommy to read you a book?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Would you like to pick or Mommy pick?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Izzy pick.

characters.name: Mistress Mouse
characters.description: A polite three-year-old mouse and resourceful shopkeeper, this community cornerstone balances a bold artistic spirit with sharp mechanical grit. Whether crafting intricate Lego creations in her sunroom or piloting her signature pink tow truck to solve puzzles and assist neighbors, she is a master of the road and a multi-talented creator. From famously adorning cars with blue stripes to dominating the race track, her inventive soul is always on the move. Usually seen in a mushroom-adorned cardboard hat with an orange sparkle purse, this resourceful driver remains an essential, evocative fixture of every neighborhood adventure.

characters.name: Olivia Mouse
characters.description: A mouse whose car got stuck and needed a tow from Mistress Mouse.

characters.name: Lily
characters.description: The world’s tallest mouse is a guardian scholar of monumental power and studious grace, serving as the living pillar of a communal house where he hoists the massive roof with peerless strength. Between shifts at the Pepperoni Street tape store and classes at the local school, this friend of Mistress Mouse finds quiet joy building with Legos or coloring in the baby lounge alongside his many brothers. Adorned in his signature shell necklace and a fresh bespoke hat from Haddy, he remains a gentle soul whose immense physical duty is matched only by his dedicated, playful heart.

characters.name: Oscar
characters.description: Hunkered in velvety shadows beside one of Mistress Mouse’s tattered stuffy mice, Lily’s brother illuminates a salvaged television with a scavenged flashlight, contentedly nibbling on oily debris. His focus drifts from the flickering screen to the miniature green car mired in a mud puddle—a casualty of the same reckless speed he employs when racing his yellow school bus. Between these high-stakes heats, he engineers sturdy Lego bridges that arch over the surrounding wreckage, all under the unblinking watch of his glass-eyed, plush sentinel.

characters.name: Alfie
characters.description: Lily’s brother, a bold infant residing in the hospital, has entered the big race behind the wheel of a heavy-duty pink tow truck. Having recently survived a thick, muddy pile-up alongside Oscar that sidelined his mouse companion’s miniature brown car, the baby now maneuvers his vibrant new rig through the ward with a champion's intent.

characters.name: Arthur
characters.description: One of Lily's brothers who plays Legos with Mistress Mouse.

characters.name: Victor
characters.description: Clad in blue shorts and a purple shirt, Lily’s brother is a tiny baby mouse and spirited cosmic stowaway who once served as a royal gardener within the Magic Forest’s glitter-shimmering houses. Now a rugged driver of a tow truck in the big car race, he balances his mechanical duties with a quest for a celestial watering can and the composition of percussive ‘bamp-bam-bum’ beats for space-faring worms. Though he treasures the sparkly art of Uncle Grisco and Haddy, his high-speed adventures often result in muddy messes that necessitate restorative baths, leaving him in singular pursuit of the perfect hat to complete his interstellar ensemble.

characters.name: Mischief Mouse
characters.description: Reigning from a purple-striped Jovian castle, this affable mouse King—a playful-named companion to Pinecone Mouse—is a seasoned adventurer, guardian of the Underground Library, and Hattie’s steadfast ally. Between savoring artisanal cheeses and awaiting a rugged cardboard replacement for his fragile Lego hat, he spends leisure hours meticulously constructing and gleefully smashing soaring towers. A pancake-loving monarch who once tended shop for mountain-dwelling traffic cones, his cosmic quests recently shifted from the stars to the speedway; having borrowed a tow truck from Mistress Mouse, he proved a formidable competitor who nearly clinched a stunning victory. Whether piloting starships or joining the group’s latest trek to Nana’s house, he remains a vigilant protector and devoted friend across the galaxy.

characters.name: Other Mouse
characters.description: A spirited mouse racer who navigates grueling long-distance courses through tangled jungles and exotic zoos with a carefree heart. Unburdened by any worldly troubles, he finds bliss in the adrenaline of the track and the quiet precision of building with Legos, living a life of seamless, architectural joy.

characters.name: Joey Giraffe
characters.description: An employee at the amusement park who calls Mistress Mouse to fix a broken merry-go-round.

places.name: Diner
places.description: A lunch spot where Mistress Mouse enjoys bacon, fruit, and potatoes with ketchup.

places.name: Lily's House
places.description: The gathering place where Mistress Mouse and her friends build a miniature Lego town.

places.name: Mexicota
places.description: A warm, sunny vacation destination with museums filled with books and blocks.

places.name: Museum
places.description: A place in Mexicota where Mistress Mouse reads Dinosaur Dance and builds block towers.

places.name: The Garage
places.description: Where Mistress Mouse keeps her pink and purple race car with five wheels.

places.name: The Race Track
places.description: A challenging course through a desert, jungle, and over a steep mountain.

places.name: Amusement Park
places.description: The site of various rides, including a merry-go-round that gets jammed by a nut bar.
